\chapter{Boolean Analysis — Depth3 Switching}
%%% generated by the blueprint pipeline from TCSlib.BooleanAnalysis.LMN.Depth3Switching
\section{Overview}

This module formalizes the switching argument for depth-3 circuits in normal form: a
depth-3 AND-of-OR-of-AND circuit is written as the AND of $s_2$ width-$w$ DNF gates.
It provides the one-step depth-3 to depth-2 compression, the two-stage switching bound
under a composed random restriction, and the resulting circuit-reduction probability
bounds, together with the CNF-cleaning machinery (tautology removal and per-clause
variable deduplication) needed to put restricted gates into normal form.

\section{Declarations}

\begin{theorem}[Restriction of a function by a composed restriction]
\lean{LMN.restrictFn_composeRestr}
\leanok
\uses{LMN.composeRestr, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction.extend, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn}
For a Boolean function $f$ and restrictions $\rho_1,\rho_2$, restricting $f$ by the
composition $\rho_1 \circ \rho_2$ equals first restricting by $\rho_1$ and then by
$\rho_2$: $\mathrm{restrictFn}\, f\,(\mathrm{composeRestr}\,\rho_1\,\rho_2) = \mathrm{restrictFn}\,(\mathrm{restrictFn}\, f\,\rho_1)\,\rho_2$.
\end{theorem}

\begin{lemma}[Decision-tree depth respects pointwise equality]
\lean{LMN.dtDepth_congr}
\leanok
\uses{dtDepth}
If two Boolean functions $f$ and $g$ agree at every input, then they have the same
decision-tree depth: $\mathrm{dtDepth}\,f = \mathrm{dtDepth}\,g$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Restriction respects pointwise equality]
\lean{LMN.restrictFn_congr}
\leanok
\uses{SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn}
If two Boolean functions $f$ and $g$ agree at every input, then for any restriction
$\rho$ their restrictions agree pointwise: $\mathrm{restrictFn}\, f\,\rho\,x = \mathrm{restrictFn}\, g\,\rho\,x$
for all $x$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{definition}[Pointwise AND of a list of functions]
\lean{LMN.listAnd}
\leanok
The pointwise conjunction of a list of Boolean functions: the empty list yields the
constant $\mathrm{true}$ function, and $f :: fs$ evaluates at $x$ to $f(x)$ AND the
value of the list $fs$ at $x$.
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}[Restriction distributes over list AND]
\lean{LMN.restrictFn_listAnd}
\leanok
\uses{LMN.listAnd, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn}
Restricting the pointwise AND of a list of functions equals the pointwise AND of the
restricted functions: for all $x$, $\mathrm{restrictFn}\,(\mathrm{listAnd}\,fs)\,\rho\,x =
\mathrm{listAnd}(fs.\mathrm{map}\,(\lambda f.\ \mathrm{restrictFn}\, f\,\rho))\,x$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{theorem}[Depth-3 one-step compression]
\lean{LMN.depth3_compression}
\leanok
\uses{CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, LMN.compression_and_of_cnfs, LMN.listAnd, LMN.one_step_reduction_with_compression, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn}
Given $s_2$ DNF gates each of width $\le w$ (with $w > 0$, non-degenerate and
duplicate-free terms) and $n > 0$, for any $0 < p \le 1/(40w)$ the Bernoulli$(p)$
random restriction satisfies, with probability at least
$1 - s_2\bigl((1/2)^l + \exp(-np/3)\bigr)$, that there exists a CNF $\Psi$ of width
$\le l$ whose evaluation equals the AND of the restricted gates. This is the depth-3
to depth-2 compression step.
\end{theorem}

\begin{definition}[Tautological clause]
\lean{LMN.clauseIsTaut}
\leanok
\uses{Literal}
A clause (a list of literals interpreted as their disjunction) is tautological if it
contains two literals on the same variable with opposite signs.
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}[A tautological clause evaluates to true]
\lean{LMN.clauseIsTaut_eval_true}
\leanok
\uses{LMN.clauseIsTaut, Literal, Literal.eval}
If a clause $c$ is tautological, then for every assignment $x$ the disjunction of its
literals evaluates to $\mathrm{true}$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{definition}[Deduplicate clause variables]
\lean{LMN.dedupClauseVars}
\leanok
\uses{Literal}
Removes duplicate variables from a clause, keeping the first occurrence of each
variable.
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}[Deduplication yields variable-injective clause]
\lean{LMN.dedupClauseVars_var_inj}
\leanok
\uses{LMN.dedupClauseVars, Literal}
In $\mathrm{dedupClauseVars}\,c$, any two literals with the same variable are equal;
that is, the resulting clause has pairwise distinct variable indices.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Deduplication yields nodup clause]
\lean{LMN.dedupClauseVars_nodup}
\leanok
\uses{LMN.dedupClauseVars, Literal}
The list $\mathrm{dedupClauseVars}\,c$ has no duplicate literals.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Deduplication does not increase length]
\lean{LMN.dedupClauseVars_length_le}
\leanok
\uses{LMN.dedupClauseVars, Literal}
The length of $\mathrm{dedupClauseVars}\,c$ is at most the length of $c$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Deduplication preserves evaluation of non-tautological clauses]
\lean{LMN.dedupClauseVars_eval_of_not_taut}
\leanok
\uses{LMN.clauseIsTaut, LMN.dedupClauseVars, Literal, Literal.eval}
If a clause $c$ is not tautological, then for every assignment $x$ the disjunction of
the literals of $\mathrm{dedupClauseVars}\,c$ has the same value as the disjunction of
the literals of $c$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{definition}[Clean a CNF]
\lean{LMN.cleanCNF_D3}
\leanok
\uses{CNF, LMN.clauseIsTaut, LMN.dedupClauseVars}
Cleans a CNF by removing all tautological clauses and then deduplicating the variables
within each remaining clause.
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}[Cleaning preserves CNF evaluation]
\lean{LMN.cleanCNF_D3_eval}
\leanok
\uses{CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.evalClause, LMN.clauseIsTaut, LMN.clauseIsTaut_eval_true, LMN.cleanCNF_D3}
For every assignment $x$, the cleaned CNF $\mathrm{cleanCNF\_D3}\,\psi$ evaluates to the
same value as $\psi$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Cleaning does not increase width]
\lean{LMN.cleanCNF_D3_width_le}
\leanok
\uses{CNF, CNF.width, LMN.cleanCNF_D3, LMN.dedupClauseVars_length_le, Term, Term.width}
The width of $\mathrm{cleanCNF\_D3}\,\psi$ is at most the width of $\psi$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Cleaned CNF has nodup clauses]
\lean{LMN.cleanCNF_D3_nodup}
\leanok
\uses{CNF, LMN.cleanCNF_D3, LMN.dedupClauseVars_nodup}
Every clause of $\mathrm{cleanCNF\_D3}\,\psi$ has no duplicate literals.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Cleaned CNF has variable-injective clauses]
\lean{LMN.cleanCNF_D3_var_inj}
\leanok
\uses{CNF, LMN.cleanCNF_D3, LMN.dedupClauseVars, LMN.dedupClauseVars_var_inj}
In every clause of $\mathrm{cleanCNF\_D3}\,\psi$, any two literals sharing the same
variable are equal.
\end{lemma}

\begin{theorem}[Every CNF has an equivalent nice CNF]
\lean{LMN.exists_nice_cnf_of_cnf}
\leanok
\uses{CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, LMN.cleanCNF_D3, LMN.cleanCNF_D3_eval, LMN.cleanCNF_D3_nodup, LMN.cleanCNF_D3_var_inj, LMN.cleanCNF_D3_width_le}
For any CNF $\psi$ there exists a CNF $\psi'$ of width $\le$ that of $\psi$, evaluating
identically to $\psi$ on every input, with no duplicate literals in any clause and with
each clause variable-injective. Hence every CNF can be cleaned to satisfy the switching
lemma hypotheses.
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[Bounded decision-tree depth gives a nice CNF]
\lean{LMN.dtDepth_le_implies_nice_cnf}
\leanok
\uses{CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, LMN.exists_nice_cnf_of_cnf, SwitchingLemma2.dtDepth_le_implies_small_dnf_cnf, dtDepth}
If a Boolean function $f$ has decision-tree depth $\le d$, then there is a CNF $\psi$ of
width $\le d$ that computes $f$, with all clauses nodup and variable-injective.
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[Bounded decision-tree depth gives a nice DNF]
\lean{LMN.dtDepth_le_implies_nice_dnf}
\leanok
\uses{DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, dtDepth}
If a Boolean function $f$ has decision-tree depth $\le d$, then there is a DNF $\varphi$
of width $\le d$ that computes $f$, with all terms nodup and variable-injective.
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[Functional switching lemma for CNFs]
\lean{LMN.switching_bernoulli_dtDepth_function}
\leanok
\uses{CNF.eval, LMN.dtDepth_congr, LMN.dtDepth_le_implies_nice_cnf, LMN.restrictFn_congr, SwitchingBernoulli.switching_bernoulli_dtDepth_cnf, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
For any function $f$ with decision-tree depth $\le w$ (with $w > 0$, $n > 0$) and any
$0 < p \le 1/(40w)$, the Bernoulli$(p)$ restriction satisfies
$\Pr_\rho[\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\, f\,\rho) > t] \le (1/2)^t + \exp(-np/3)$.
This is the switching lemma stated at the level of functions rather than formulas.
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[General two-stage bound]
\lean{LMN.two_stage_bound}
\leanok
\uses{LMN.composeRestr, LMN.restriction_compose_eq, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb_le_one', SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrWeight, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrWeight_nonneg', SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrWeight_sum_one}
Let $0 < p_1,p_2 \le 1$, let $E$ be an event on restrictions and $A$ a ``failure''
event, and let $\beta \ge 0$. If for every $\rho_1$ with $\lnot A(\rho_1)$ the
conditional probability $\Pr_{p_2}[E(\mathrm{composeRestr}\,\rho_1\,\rho_2)] \le \beta$, then
$\Pr_{p_1 p_2}[E] \le \Pr_{p_1}[A] + \beta$.
\end{theorem}

\begin{lemma}[AND of switched gates has a nice CNF]
\lean{LMN.and_of_gates_has_cnf}
\leanok
\uses{CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, DNF, DNF.eval, LMN.compression_and_of_cnfs, LMN.exists_nice_cnf_of_cnf, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
If each restricted gate $\mathrm{restrictFn}\,(\mathrm{gates}\,i).\mathrm{eval}\,\rho_1$ has
decision-tree depth $\le l$, then there is a CNF $\Psi$ of width $\le l$, with nodup and
variable-injective clauses, whose evaluation equals the AND over all gates of their
restricted evaluations.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Restricted depth-3 circuit has a nice CNF]
\lean{LMN.depth3_restricted_has_nice_cnf}
\leanok
\uses{CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, DNF, DNF.eval, LMN.and_of_gates_has_cnf, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction.extend, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
Suppose $f$ is the AND of the gates ($f(x) = \mathrm{true}$ iff every gate is true at
$x$) and each restricted gate has decision-tree depth $\le l$. Then there is a CNF
$\Psi$ of width $\le l$, with nodup and variable-injective clauses, whose evaluation
equals $\mathrm{restrictFn}\, f\,\rho_1$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Second-stage switching bound]
\lean{LMN.depth3_second_stage_bound}
\leanok
\uses{CNF.eval, DNF, DNF.eval, LMN.cleanCNF_D3, LMN.cleanCNF_D3_eval, LMN.cleanCNF_D3_nodup, LMN.cleanCNF_D3_var_inj, LMN.cleanCNF_D3_width_le, LMN.composeRestr, LMN.depth3_restricted_has_nice_cnf, LMN.dtDepth_congr, LMN.restrictFn_composeRestr, LMN.restrictFn_congr, SwitchingBernoulli.switching_bernoulli_dtDepth_cnf, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
Suppose $f$ is the AND of the gates and, under the first-stage restriction $\rho_1$,
each restricted gate has decision-tree depth $\le l$ (with $l > 0$, $n > 0$). Then for
any $0 < p_2 \le 1/(40l)$ the second-stage Bernoulli$(p_2)$ restriction satisfies
$\Pr_{\rho_2}[\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\, f\,(\mathrm{composeRestr}\,\rho_1\,\rho_2)) > t]
\le (1/2)^t + \exp(-np_2/3)$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{theorem}[Depth-3 two-stage switching bound]
\lean{LMN.depth3_switching_bound}
\leanok
\uses{DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, LMN.depth3_second_stage_bound, LMN.switching_bernoulli_union_bound, LMN.two_stage_bound, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
Let $f$ be the AND of $s_2$ DNF gates each of width $\le w$ (with $w,l > 0$,
non-degenerate and duplicate-free terms, $n > 0$). For $0 < p_1 \le 1/(40w)$ and
$0 < p_2 \le 1/(40l)$, the composed Bernoulli$(p_1 p_2)$ restriction satisfies
\[
\Pr_\rho[\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\, f\,\rho) > t]
\le s_2\bigl((1/2)^l + \exp(-np_1/3)\bigr) + \bigl((1/2)^t + \exp(-np_2/3)\bigr).
\]
\end{theorem}

\begin{lemma}[Depth-3 circuit reduction bound]
\lean{LMN.circuit_reduction_depth3}
\leanok
\uses{DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, LMN.composedDelta, LMN.depth3_switching_bound, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
For a depth-3 circuit in normal form (AND of $s_2$ width-$w$ DNF gates, with $w,l > 0$,
$n > 0$), under the composed Bernoulli$(\mathrm{composedDelta}\,w\,l\,3) =
\mathrm{Bernoulli}(1/(40w) \cdot 1/(40l))$ restriction, writing $p_1 = 1/(40w)$ and
$p_2 = 1/(40l)$,
\[
\Pr_\rho[\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\, f\,\rho) > t]
\le s_2\bigl((1/2)^l + \exp(-np_1/3)\bigr) + \bigl((1/2)^t + \exp(-np_2/3)\bigr).
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{theorem}[Depth-3 switching lemma, $\le \varepsilon$ version]
\lean{LMN.circuit_reduction_depth3_le_eps}
\leanok
\uses{DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, LMN.circuit_reduction_depth3, LMN.composedDelta, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
For a depth-3 circuit in normal form, if $l$ and $t$ are chosen so that
$s_2 (1/2)^l \le \varepsilon/2$ and $(1/2)^t \le \varepsilon/2$, then under the
composed Bernoulli$(\mathrm{composedDelta}\,w\,l\,3)$ restriction (with $p_1 = 1/(40w)$,
$p_2 = 1/(40l)$),
\[
\Pr_\rho[\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\, f\,\rho) > t]
\le \varepsilon + s_2\exp(-np_1/3) + \exp(-np_2/3).
\]
The exponential tails vanish as $n \to \infty$, so the bound is $\le \varepsilon$
asymptotically.
\end{theorem}
